title: Watsonia aletroides
intro: Watsonia aletroides, commonly known as Cape Watsonia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Iridaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Watsonia aletroides is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Watsonia aletroides include flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional South African medicine, Watsonia aletroides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can also be found in capsule or tincture form, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies.

The Iridaceae family is further classified into several subfamilies, and Watsonia aletroides falls under the subfamily Iridoideae.
